rationale: Re-verified the R4 approval lens against 18d7ea3f. `go build ./...` exits 0, with only a sandbox write warning for Go's module stat cache. The exact `deadcode@latest` command cannot run here because DNS for proxy.golang.org is unavailable; cached-source fallback also cannot build offline due missing dependency metadata, so I do not claim a fresh deadcode result. Source greps still support the spec: the renumbered R1 list maps to the current pre-implementation dead/deprecated symbols, `internal/setup/codex.go` still has the claimed managed-doc `os.WriteFile`/`os.OpenFile` writes at 68/79/96, and `AGENTS.md` still lacks the guardrails section while `CLAUDE.md` references it. Round-2 changes correctly defer `.golangci.yml` carve-out removal and update the children-query test seam language. The prior R4 wording nit remains non-blocking.

findings.observed: `internal/setup/codex.go` contains the claimed managed `AGENTS.md` writes through plain `os.WriteFile` / `os.OpenFile`; `AGENTS.md` has no `## Bead-loop guardrails (mindspec)` section while `CLAUDE.md` points to it; the renumbered requirements now exclude `.golangci.yml` and defer those stale carve-outs to wave 2.

`go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@latest -test ./cmd/... ./internal/... ./plugins/...` failed before analysis: DNS lookup for `proxy.golang.org` failed. Attempting cached `x/tools@v0.47.0` source also failed offline because dependency metadata for `github.com/google/go-cmp@v0.6.0` was unavailable.
findings.impact: Could not produce a fresh tool listing in this sandbox. Manual `rg` cross-check found no tree contradiction against the spec's R1 deletion list.

findings.observed: The round-1 R4 nit remains: AC #1 still names `internal/harness` seams and deferred `internal/trace/event.go` `Event.MarshalJSON` as exceptions to the `deadcode -test` proof. Prior R4 execution at c7c70000 found those exceptions did not appear under `-test`.
findings.impact: Benign and over-permissive rather than unsafe. After the R1 deletions, a zero-finding `-test` run would still satisfy the criterion.
